result:
There is a specific kind of magic that exists only in the hours before dawn. The world is shrouded in a heavy, indigo blanket, and the relentless noise of the day has yet to begin. In this quiet space, time feels elastic, stretching out like a cat lounging in a patch of sunlight. There are no emails to answer, no traffic to navigate, no expectations pressing against the edges of the mind. There is only the soft rhythm of your own breathing and the faint, distant hum of a world still dreaming. 

I have always found a deep comfort in this liminal space. It feels like standing in the wings of a theater just before the curtain rises. The props are set, the stage is dark, and the actors are still in the wings. Anything is possible. The day ahead is an unwritten script, free from the edits and revisions of reality. 

As the minutes tick by, the sky begins to betray the sunrise. A thin line of bruised purple appears on the horizon, slowly bleeding into shades of apricot and gold. The birds are the first to announce the transition, their tentative chirps building into a full symphony. The magic doesn't vanish entirely as the light washes over the world, but it changes form, trading its quiet mystery for the vibrant, chaotic pulse of the waking. Still, carrying just a fraction of that pre-dawn stillness in your chest can serve as a perfect anchor for whatever comes next.
